_description: Synthetic CoW Protocol Settlement batch that superficially looks like F-V-B. The 'attacker' address is CoW Protocol Settlement (0x9008D19f58AAbD9eD0D60971565AA8510560ab41). Three swaps on the same pool in the same block — structurally matching F-V-B — but all originated from the settlement contract. The D13 settlement allowlist must hard-suppress this candidate. Expected: sandwich_mev_v1 must NOT fire.
_source: Synthetic — CoW Protocol settlement batch mimicking sandwich structure. CoW batch settlement contract address 0x9008D19f58AAbD9eD0D60971565AA8510560ab41 is from CoW Protocol GitHub (https://github.com/cowprotocol/contracts) and Etherscan-verified. Legitimate CoW batch settlement produces F-V-B-like swap sequences as a side effect of batch clearing. Per D13 Decision D-7 (ADR 0003): hard-suppress all events where attacker address is in the settlement allowlist.
_adr_note: ADR 0003 carve-out: settlement allowlist is hardcoded (no runtime API). Synthetic fixture verifies suppression logic without live RPC.

_expected.reason: attacker_address in settlement_allowlist (CoW Protocol Settlement). Hard-suppressed per D13 Decision D-7 / ADR 0003.

_notes: The three swaps structurally match the F-V-B pattern (same pool, same block, direction consistent).
The sender of swaps [0] and [2] is the CoW Protocol Settlement contract — a legitimate batch clearing contract.
CoW Settlement produces F-V-B-like swap sequences as a side effect of uniform-price batch clearing.
D13 detect_sandwich_pattern() WILL find a candidate here (structural match).
D13 evaluate_inner() MUST suppress the candidate at the allowlist check (before confidence scoring).
SPEC-NOTE: CoW contract address sourced from design 0021 §1.3. Verify against CoW GitHub Sprint 21.
